content.problemStatement:
In ShareChat, there is a sequence of binary integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You should answer QQQ queries on this sequence. In each query, you are given two indices LLL and RRR and a binary integer XXX.

The answer to a query is the smallest index iii (L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R) such that the value Ai‚äïXA_i \oplus XAi‚Äã‚äïX is maximum possible, i.e. equal to max(AL‚äïX,AL+1‚äïX,‚Ä¶,AR‚äïX)\mathrm{max}(A_L \oplus X, A_{L+1} \oplus X, \ldots, A_R \oplus X)max(AL‚Äã‚äïX,AL+1‚Äã‚äïX,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã‚äïX). Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
constraints: the sum of lengths of strings denoting A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of lengths of strings denoting XXX over all queries does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (33 points):** all binary strings on the input have equal lengths
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains a single binary string denoting the number AiA_iAi‚Äã (in binary representation).
The following QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers LLL and RRR, followed by a space and a binary string denoting the number XXX (in binary representation).
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the answer to that query.
